perturbation.original_text: Distributor will use its best efforts aggressively to develop sales of the Product in the Territory. In furtherance thereof, Distributor will: (a) keep on hand a reasonable inventory of the Product sufficient to allow for prompt delivery of the Product to resellers; (b) establish a program to market the Product, including, but not limited to, participating regularly in local and regional trade shows, conventions or like events in the Territory, and conducting regular local promotional and other marketing efforts for the Product; (c) provide quality product support to resellers, including, but not limited to, providing appropriate installation and application advice and prompt follow-up service and advice to resellers of the Product upon request; (d) provide a support center to resellers, including, but not limited to, a hotline service to answer reseller questions and to receive and track complaints and any reports of claimed errors in the Product; 3 (e) provide quality product technical and sales training to resellers; (f) respond promptly to sales leads or referrals furnished by WGT or by other distributors or dealers of WGT; (g) have a designated number of employees attend such technical and sales training programs as set forth in Exhibit C; (h) maintain and furnish periodically, as WGT may reasonably request, complete and accurate records of each sale or other distribution of each Product sold or distributed by Distributor (e.g., showing the date of sale, Zip code of the customer, the Product serial number and the applicable Product license key(s)) under this Agreement; (i) promptly advise WGT of each complaint that Distributor may receive or becomes aware of concerning the Product or any portion thereof (including, but not limited to, warranty claims). Distributor will promptly investigate all such complaints and will give immediate attention to and use its best efforts to promptly, courteously and equitably respond to, adjust and settle (without incurring any obligation or liability on behalf of WGT) all complaints received by Distributor from any customer, potential customer or anyone else arising out of or in connection with Distributor's sale of any Product, or the performance of any services. In handling any complaints, Distributor will use its best efforts to maintain and promote good public relations for WGT; (j) secure and maintain, in the name of WGT, any and all registrations, permits, licenses, approvals and other governmental actions required to import, handle, market, sell, demonstrate, use and distribute the Product in the Territory, provide to WGT quarterly progress reports on such action, and provide WGT copies of all registrations, permits, licenses, approvals, certificates, correspondence and other documentation related to such action; (k) hire, train, coordinate and maintain a qualified staff of sufficient size and with a level and mix of capabilities as are reasonably necessary to accomplish the goals contemplated under this Agreement; (l) avoid deceptive, misleading or unethical conduct which are or might be detrimental to WGT or its Product, and refrain from making any representation, warranty or guarantee to any reseller with respect to specifications, features or capabilities of the Product that is inconsistent with the literature distributed by WGT or this Agreement; (m) conduct its business in a manner under its own control, provided that Distributor will at all times comply with all applicable laws and regulations and will not engage in, or permit its employees or agents to engage in, any activities or practices which could reflect negatively upon the reputation or prospects of WGT or the Product or expose WGT to any liability of any nature whatsoever;
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor will endeavor to develop sales of the Product in the Territory. In furtherance thereof, Distributor will: (a) attempt to keep a reasonable inventory of the Product to allow for delivery of the Product to resellers; (b) initiate a program to market the Product, including, but not limited to, occasionally participating in trade shows; (c) endeavor to provide product support to resellers; (d) consider providing a support center to resellers; 3 (e) attempt to provide product technical and sales training to resellers; (f) try to respond to sales leads or referrals furnished by WGT or by other distributors or dealers of WGT; (g) aim to have a reasonable number of employees attend training programs as set forth in Exhibit C; (h) maintain records of each sale or other distribution of each Product sold or distributed by Distributor; (i) advise WGT of each complaint that Distributor may receive or becomes aware of concerning the Product or any portion thereof, and give attention to complaints received by Distributor from any customer. In handling any complaints, Distributor will endeavor to maintain good public relations for WGT; (j) secure registrations, permits, licenses, approvals, and other governmental actions required to import, handle, market, sell, demonstrate, use and distribute the Product in the Territory and provide progress reports on such action; (k) hire, train, coordinate and maintain a staff of sufficient size to accomplish the goals contemplated under this Agreement; (l) avoid conduct which might be detrimental to WGT or its Product, and refrain from making any representation to any reseller with respect to specifications, features or capabilities of the Product that is inconsistent with the literature distributed by WGT or this Agreement; (m) conduct its business in a manner under its own control, provided that Distributor will comply with applicable laws and regulations and will not engage in any activities or practices which could reflect negatively upon the reputation or prospects of WGT or the Product or expose WGT to any liability.
perturbation.explanation: The modifications replace mandatory obligations with weaker, discretionary actions. Phrases like 'use its best efforts' are replaced with 'endeavor to', 'keep on hand' becomes 'attempt to keep,' and 'maintain and furnish periodically' is simplified to 'maintain records.' This creates ambiguity about the level of effort and commitment required, potentially conflicting with consumer protection laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: The changes make the distributor's obligations regarding product quality and support ambiguous. This conflicts with the Consumer Rights Act 2015, which requires goods to be of satisfactory quality, fit for purpose, and as described. The modified text weakens the distributor's responsibility, potentially leaving consumers without adequate recourse if products are faulty or misrepresented.

perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates specific actions by the distributor to develop sales and support the product, using terms like 'will use its best efforts,' 'keep on hand,' and 'maintain and furnish periodically.' The modified contract replaces these with weaker, discretionary language such as 'endeavor to,' 'attempt to keep,' and 'maintain records.' This shift creates ambiguity and reduces the distributor's obligations. The Law Explanation suggests this weakens the distributor's responsibility regarding product quality and support, potentially conflicting with consumer protection laws requiring goods to be of satisfactory quality and as described. The scraped law snippet mentions the 'Consumer Rights Act 2015', implying a legal standard for consumer protection, which the weakened obligations in the modified contract may fail to meet by creating ambiguities around product support and quality responsibilities. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguity Legal Contradiction, where obligations are introduced ambiguously, making them difficult to enforce.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor will place orders for the Product from WGT by completing, signing and submitting to WGT a written order for the same, in a form acceptable to WGT, via facsimile, mail or other means. Distributor shall submit such order at least thirty (30) days in advance of the delivery date set forth in each order. All orders will be subject to acceptance by WGT through written acceptance or shipment of the Product subject to the order.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor may place orders for the Product from WGT by submitting an order via available means. Distributor should aim to submit such order in advance of the suggested delivery date. All orders will be subject to acceptance by WGT.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a clear process for order placement, including written orders, a 30-day advance notice, and acceptance by WGT. The modified version introduces ambiguity by replacing 'will' with 'may,' 'shall' with 'should aim to,' and removing the requirement for written orders and specifying '30 days in advance'.
perturbation.contradicted_law: United Nations Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods (CISG)
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 14 of the CISG dictates requirements for an offer, including certainty and indication of intent to be bound. By making the ordering process discretionary and removing the specificity regarding order format and lead time, the modified text creates uncertainty around the terms of an offer, making it harder to establish a binding contract for international sales under the CISG, specifically between a US-based company and a European distributor, creating potential non-compliance issues if the contract is interpreted under CISG.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: § 2-206. Offer and Acceptance in Formation of Contract. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-206. Offer and Acceptance in Formation of Contract. (1) Unless otherwise unambiguously indicated by the language or circumstances (a) an offer to make a contract shall be construed as inviting acceptance in any manner and by any medium reasonable in the circumstances; (b) an order or other offer to buy goods for prompt or current shipment shall be construed as inviting acceptance either by a prompt promise to ship or by the prompt or current shipment of conforming or non-conforming goods, but such a shipment of non-conforming goods does not constitute an acceptance if the seller seasonably notifies the buyer that the shipment is offered only as an accommodation to the buyer. (2) Where the beginning of a requested performance is a reasonable mode of acceptance an offeror who is not notified of acceptance within a reasonable time may treat the offer as having lapsed before acceptance. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The original contract specifies a clear process for order placement, while the changed version introduces ambiguity. The law explanation states that the change creates uncertainty around the terms of an offer. UCC 2-206 discusses offer and acceptance in contract formation, requiring clarity. By making the ordering process discretionary ('may' instead of 'will,' 'should aim' instead of 'shall'), the modified text introduces ambiguity that could lead to disputes about whether a valid offer was made. The original text's specific requirements (written order, 30-day notice) provided certainty, whereas the modified version lacks this clarity, thus contradicting the need for a clear offer as per UCC 2-206.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor will comply with the trademark guidelines and procedures established by WGT in Distributor's use of the Trademarks including without limitation use of the trademark and copyright symbols as specified by WGT from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor will consider the trademark guidelines and procedures provided by WGT in Distributor's use of the Trademarks.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates compliance with WGT's trademark guidelines, ensuring proper use of trademarks and copyright symbols. The modified version weakens this requirement by replacing 'will comply' with 'will consider'.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Lanham Act protects trademark owners from unauthorized use of their marks. By making compliance with trademark guidelines discretionary, the modification increases the risk of trademark misuse or infringement. This contradicts the Lanham Act's intent to ensure proper use and protection of trademarks, as the distributor might use the trademarks in a way that harms the brand or confuses consumers.

perturbation.justification: The original text mandates compliance ('will comply') with WGT's trademark guidelines, whereas the changed text weakens this obligation to mere consideration ('will consider'). This introduces ambiguity regarding the distributor's responsibility to adhere to the trademark guidelines. The Law Explanation states that this modification increases the risk of trademark misuse or infringement. Snippet 2 mentions 'Remedies; infringement' and 'False designations of origin, false descriptions, and dilution forbidden,' implying that there are legal consequences for trademark infringement, which the original contract sought to prevent. The change undermines the protection afforded to trademark owners under trademark law, creating a contradiction. The "Ambiguities - Ambiguous Legal Obligation" definition states that 'A legal contradiction under this category happens when an obligation is introduced ambiguously, making it difficult to enforce under state or national law'. Therefore, replacing 'will comply' with 'will consider' makes the obligation ambiguous and more difficult to enforce.
